pending.body: Make current-version pid records authoritative on non-Linux Unix. `write_pid_file` records `proc_start_ticks: None` whenever `/proc/<pid>/stat` is unavailable (`src/adapters/fs.rs:284-295`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:670-680`), and both `is_pid_alive` and `classify_running_snapshot_liveness` reject those records as non-authoritative (`src/adapters/fs.rs:329-347`, `src/cli/run.rs:1533-1547`). On macOS/BSD that means every current run falls back to the legacy lease path, so a dead orchestrator keeps reporting as running until the lease TTL expires, `run resume` refuses stale recovery (`src/cli/run.rs:1618-1624`), and `run stop` rejects the run as a legacy CLI run (`src/cli/run.rs:2378-2379`). Add a non-`/proc` process identity check for non-Linux Unix, or another way to mark current pid records as authoritative there.

Do not reclaim an observed writer-lock owner for ordinary `Failed`/`Paused` resumes unless that owner has been proven stale. `observed_resume_recovery_owner` records whatever lock owner exists even for terminal snapshots (`src/cli/run.rs:1635-1637`), and `acquire_recovery_writer_guard` unconditionally calls `reclaim_specific_project_writer_owner` before trying to acquire its own lease (`src/cli/run.rs:1579-1594`, `src/cli/run.rs:2230-2238`). If another `run resume` or daemon worker wins the race after we read `run.json`, this path will tear down that live owner and can even mark the live worktree task failed (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/cli_writer_lease.rs:123-175`) before we later discover the snapshot is already `Running`. Reclaim should only happen after stale-owner proof, or after a normal acquire fails and the existing owner is independently verified stale.

`git diff` for this revision only touches `.ralph-burning/projects/rlm/journal.ndjson` (around lines 237–243) and `.ralph-burning/projects/rlm/run.json` (around lines 12–29). No source files under `src/` are modified, so required runtime changes for PID lifecycle tracking (`src/adapters/fs.rs`), `run stop`/stale handling (`src/cli/run.rs`), and resume recovery/signal-safe interruption (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs`) are not implemented in this patch.
